comment.body: It's not nearly that simple..at least if you're trying to get ahead of market trends. When gamma is low, volatility impact is high. And if you look at OD charts or Alma’s charts, you'll see that prices often bounce right off negative gamma bars. Alma explained this using vanna convexity, and Volsignals also referenced her study in one of his morning meetings last year
comment.raw_body: It's not nearly that simple..at least if you're trying to get ahead of market trends. When gamma is low, volatility impact is high. comment.body: Positive gamma, on the other hand, can act either as a vacuum effect or as a repelling force, depending on volatility and other flows. Alma always teaches that alpha exists where there’s mispricing. And that’s exactly how we operate in the industry.
